examples: A titanic snake has been identified as the top predator to walk, or at least slither, the land when the dinosaurs disappeared.
What those early craftsmen had found by trial and error was that under pressure a pagoda's loose stack of floors could be made to slither to and fro independent of one another.
He can slither down from a sofa, pick up peas and point wildly at anything that catches his bright blue eyes.
What joints there are between the floors are loosely fitting wooden brackets that allow each storey to slither around.
Environmentalists are worried that the UK might slither back from its international commitment to create an ecologically coherent network of sites.
